title: Market Brief — Thursday, May 14, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100s at 70%, and MI300X at 60%, while cutting-edge silicon is locked tight with H200 at 45%, B200 at just 25% (scarce), and GB200 at 10% (severe shortage). B200 utilization surged 13.9pp in 30 days to 85.9% and H100 hit 100%, signaling demand is rapidly absorbing next-gen supply even as legacy hardware floods the market.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100s at 70%, and MI300X at 60%, while cutting-edge silicon is locked tight with H200 at 45%, B200 at just 25% (scarce), and GB200 at 10% (severe shortage). B200 utilization surged 13.9pp in 30 days to 85.9% and H100 hit 100%, signaling demand is rapidly absorbing next-gen supply even as legacy hardware floods the market.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), Llama 70B inference runs roughly $9.47/M on A100, $9.84/M on H100, and $11.90/M on MI300X, meaning any provider pricing output below $10/M on those GPUs is burning cash. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output and Meta Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60/M are selling at losses exceeding $9/M tokens, subsidizing adoption. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output and Anthropic Opus 4.6 at $25/M are the only models with clear premium-tier margins.

B200 spot at $4.20/hr against OCPI of $5.08/hr with 108% annualized volatility looks undervalued; accumulate B200 compute contracts before availability tightens further below 25%. A100s at 85% availability and flat utilization are overvalued legacy iron; sell or redeploy.
bullets: 10% availability on GB200 marks the tightest compute class in the stack, signaling severe shortage.
85% A100 availability with only 0.6pp utilization gain in 30 days confirms oversupply in legacy GPU.
$0.378/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a loss of roughly $9.09/M tokens versus TCO-adjusted H100 cost.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means frontier output pricing has fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch.
108.3% annualized volatility on B200 OCPI is the highest of any GPU class tracked.
$80/M output on o3 Pro makes it 211x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2 per output token.
